sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Standard Liège have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a recent narrow 2-1 victory over Cercle Brugge. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, on the other hand, are in a slump, with no wins in their last 4 matches, managing only 1 draw and 3 losses, conceding in every game. This suggests Standard Liège hold a form advantage heading into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Oud-Heverlee Leuven have had the upper hand, with a 1-0 win in August 2025 and a 1-1 draw in April 2025. Historically, Leuven have not lost to Standard Liège in their last 7 meetings, which could provide them with a psychological edge despite their poor recent form.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Standard Liège, sitting 6th with 24 points, have a decent defensive record (7th least goals conceded) but struggle offensively, ranking 14th in goals scored (16). Oud-Heverlee Leuven, languishing at 14th with 16 points, have conceded 27 goals, one of the league's worst defenses, while also scoring only 18. Standard Liège's defensive solidity might be key against Leuven’s leaky backline.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Standard Liège, Rafiki Saïd (7.04 avg. rating) and Dennis Eckert Ayensa (7.36 avg. rating vs. Leuven historically) could be pivotal in attack. Leuven’s Łukasz Łakomy, in form with 2 goals and 2 assists in the last 5 matches, poses a threat in midfield and will be crucial for any upset chances.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Considering Sofascore’s prediction (45% home win, 31% draw, 24% away win), Standard Liège’s better league position, and Leuven’s ongoing winless streak, the hosts are favored to win. However, Leuven’s head-to-head dominance suggests a tight contest, potentially leading to a low-scoring game as seen in recent matchups (under 2.5 goals in last 5 H2H).

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Standard Liège and Oud-Heverlee Leuven ended in a goalless draw at the Maurice Dufrasne Stadium. Neither side managed to break the deadlock, with both teams showing moments of promise but lacking the cutting edge in the final third. Standard Liège held their ground defensively while trying to create openings, whereas Leuven controlled possession more effectively and created a notable chance but failed to convert.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven dominated possession in the first half and showed greater intent in terms of final third entries. They also created a significant opportunity but missed the target when it mattered. Standard Liège, on the other hand, were slightly more balanced in duels and showed resilience in defense, managing to restrict Leuven’s attacking output despite less time on the ball. Both teams recorded an equal number of efforts on target, indicating a tight contest so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Standard Liège started with intent, pushing forward in the early stages with a series of promising attacks. However, momentum shifted toward Oud-Heverlee Leuven around the midway point of the half, as they sustained pressure with several dangerous spells in the opposition half. Towards the end of the half, Leuven continued to press, while Standard Liège had brief moments of resurgence but couldn’t maintain consistent threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Standard Liège, Dennis Eckert Ayensa stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half, given his involvement in the attack during the first 45 minutes. Tobias Mohr also impressed with his ability to create opportunities and could be pivotal in breaking down Leuven’s defense. On the Leuven side, Sory Kaba has been a threat and instrumental in creating chances, making him a likely candidate to influence the outcome. Youssef Maziz could also play a key role with his creativity in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège will likely focus on improving their attacking efficiency, as they’ve shown capability in entering dangerous areas but need better decision-making in the final moments. They may push for more direct play to exploit any defensive lapses from Leuven. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, with their possession advantage, might look to maintain control and capitalize on set-pieces or quick transitions to finally convert their chances. Both teams could introduce fresh legs to inject energy into the game.
